query: Norovirus GII.17 wastewater levels spring summer 2026 decline trajectory United States
answer:
Excellent — both agents returned rich data. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

---

# Norovirus GII.17 Wastewater Levels & Decline Trajectory — United States, Spring/Summer 2026

## Executive Summary

Norovirus GII.17[P17], which dominated the 2024–2025 season at ~74.5% of genotyped outbreaks, has **dramatically declined** in the 2025–2026 season and is no longer the dominant circulating strain. GII.4 Sydney[P16] has reasserted dominance at 52.0%. The overall 2025–2026 norovirus season peaked in early February 2026 and has been in steady decline since, with activity now approaching the seasonal summer trough. Wastewater levels are inferred to be at "low" or "minimal" categorical levels nationally as of early June 2026.

---

## 1. GII.17 Genotype Shift

GII.17[P17] experienced an extraordinary rise and fall:

| Metric | 2024–2025 Season | 2025–2026 Season (through Apr 30, 2026) |
|---|---|---|
| **GII.17[P17] share** | **74.5%** (306 of 411 outbreaks) | **19.6%** (20 of 102 outbreaks) |
| **GII.4 Sydney[P16] share** | 10.5% (43 outbreaks) | **52.0%** (53 outbreaks) |
| **GII.7[P7] share** | — | 6.9% (7 outbreaks) |

- GII.17[P17] dropped roughly **4× in proportional share** between seasons.
- GII.4 Sydney[P16] regained its historical dominance, consistent with long-term epidemiological patterns.
- **Data source:** CDC CaliciNet, last updated **May 12, 2026**.

## 2. Seasonal Outbreak Trajectory (CDC NoroSTAT, 14 states)

The 2025–2026 season peaked in early February and has been declining steeply:

| Period | Weekly Outbreaks | Context |
|---|---|---|
| Aug–Sep 2025 | 4–13/week | Low baseline |
| Oct 31, 2025 | **36/week** | New historical weekly max for that calendar week |
| Nov–Dec 2025 | 15–55/week | Seasonal ramp-up, within IQR |
| Jan 30, 2026 | **68/week** | Exceeded 75th percentile (61) |
| **Feb 6, 2026 (PEAK)** | **78/week** | Season peak; above 75th pctl (63), near historical max (117) |
| Mar 2026 | 33–41/week | Declining, approaching 25th percentile |
| Apr 2026 | 22–28/week | At or below IQR |
| **May 1–7, 2026** (latest) | **19/week** | Baseline summer levels |

### Cumulative Season Totals

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| 2025–2026 cumulative outbreaks (Aug 1 – May 7) | **1,194** |
| 2024–2025 cumulative (same period) | **2,534** |
| Year-over-year decline | **−53%** (1,340 fewer outbreaks) |
| vs. historical range (2012–2025) | **Within IQR** (2024–2025 was the anomaly) |

**Data last updated:** May 20, 2026 (through May 7, 2026). Next update expected ~June 17–20, 2026.

## 3. Historical June/July Baseline (CDC NoroSTAT, 2012–2025)

June represents the **annual summer trough** for norovirus:

| Week | Min | 25th Pctl | 75th Pctl | Max |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jun 5 | 0 | 7 | 9 | 13 |
| Jun 12 | 2 | 7 | 9 | 13 |
| Jun 19 | 1 | 6 | 11 | 14 |
| Jun 26 | 1 | 4 | 7 | 11 |
| Jul 3 | 1 | 4 | 7 | 11 |
| Jul 17 | 0 | 4 | 9 | 11 |

**Typical May–July cumulative additions:** ~100–170 outbreaks, representing ~8–12% of full-season totals.

## 4. Wastewater Surveillance Status

Direct access to the CDC NWSS dashboard and WastewaterSCAN dashboard was not achieved during this research. However, based on clinical outbreak data:

- **Inferred wastewater categorical level (early June 2026):** "Low" or "Minimal" nationally
- **Rationale:** The season peaked 4 months ago; weekly outbreaks are at 19/week and heading into the historical summer nadir (4–14 outbreaks/week in June). Wastewater signals track closely with clinical data and would be expected to show corresponding low/minimal concentrations.
- **WastewaterSCAN categorical levels:** Minimal → Low → Moderate → High → Very High

## 5. U.S. Norovirus Burden (Standing CDC Estimates)

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Annual illnesses | 19–21 million |
| Annual hospitalizations | ~109,000 |
| Annual deaths | ~900 (mostly ≥65 years) |
| Average annual reported outbreaks | ~2,500 |
| Share of all U.S. foodborne illness | 58% |
| Annual economic cost | ~$2 billion |

## 6. Key Takeaways for Trajectory Assessment

1. **GII.17 is no longer dominant** — it has declined from 74.5% to 19.6% of genotyped outbreaks between seasons.
2. **The overall norovirus season is in its terminal decline phase**, with activity at summer baseline levels as of early May 2026.
3. **The 2025–2026 season has been 53% less active** than the anomalous GII.17-driven 2024–2025 season, and sits within the normal historical interquartile range.
4. **June/July historically represent the annual nadir** — further declines from the current 19/week to 4–9/week are expected.
5. **Wastewater levels are inferred to be at low/minimal levels** nationally, consistent with the clinical decline trajectory.

---

key_facts: GII.17[P17] declined from 74.5% of genotyped outbreaks in 2024-2025 to 19.6% in 2025-2026 (through April 30, 2026) — a roughly 4× drop in proportional share.
GII.4 Sydney[P16] has regained dominance at 52.0% of genotyped outbreaks in 2025-2026, up from 10.5% the prior season.
The 2025-2026 norovirus season peaked on February 6, 2026 at 78 outbreaks/week (CDC NoroSTAT, 14 states).
Weekly outbreaks declined from 78/week (Feb peak) to 19/week (May 1-7, 2026) — a 76% decline from peak.
Cumulative 2025-2026 season outbreaks: 1,194 (through May 7, 2026), compared to 2,534 in 2024-2025 — a 53% year-over-year decline.
The 2025-2026 season is within the historical interquartile range (2012-2025); the 2024-2025 GII.17-dominated season was the anomaly.
Historical June baseline: 4-14 outbreaks/week across NoroSTAT states; June represents the annual summer trough.
Wastewater surveillance levels are inferred to be at 'low' or 'minimal' categorical levels nationally in early June 2026, consistent with clinical data trends.
CDC CaliciNet genotype data last updated May 12, 2026; CDC NoroSTAT outbreak data last updated May 20, 2026 (through May 7).
Next CDC NoroSTAT data update expected around June 17-20, 2026.
